texts.full_text: ver time – trusting the unfolding process will yield better results. Art doesn't just grow from ideas formed in a blink but is nurtured through continuous attention, which sometimes includes making tweaks mid-process. Each change might appear to be slight on surface level yet may greatly influence an artwork's depth and longevity. Ultimately art-making has less about speed than it does trusting one’s vision or skill will flourish. It suggests there should not overcompel the progress – growth takes its time, just as a sunflower stretches towards sunlight. This process might feel unending for those eager to see immediate outcomes from initial ideas. To sum up: Trust in yourself and give your art all it needs without rushing toward that end goal too fast. The artwork’s essence will evolve slowly like roots breaking ground while seeking water, eventually turning the artist's original vision into something more refined than initially envisioned. This process requires patience which leads to a work of true quality. And so I think with you… Let your creative ideas grow gradually and naturally – let them shape themselves without being rushed or unduly hasted. Don’t rush towards completing it as if pushing can get the final result quicker than nature would allow. The artist hopes for an appreciation toward time spent refining artwork over eagerly seeking instant perfection, which could miss out on necessary stages leading up to becoming something remarkable. This journey requires endurance but will ultimately reward artists with fulfillment. In trusting patience and enduring moments of doubt or instability – the greatest masterpieces often emerge from such experiences. It's in these slow phases that growth takes place amidst a shifting terrain where there might still be much work left for improvement yet is important to hold on during turbulent stages, knowing they lead towards new possibilities. texts.full_text: in Iowa drinking water and their effects on human health. A number of studies suggest links between elevated nitrate concentrations in drinking water and other health issues, including birth defects, cancers, thyroid problems and a variety of other health concerns. In order to protect against blue-baby syndrome, the EPA’s maximum contaminant level or MCL is set at 10 mg/liter. Since blue-baby syndrome has not been seen as a problem of late, there has been a pressure to raise the limit and allow more nitrate in drinking water. However, there is now a pushback from the public health community to look at the studies that show a definite health risk. One is the Iowa Women’s Health study of 2200 women, over twenty years, drinking water with known nitrate levels. Even with levels of 2.5 mg/liter, there was a 2 - 3 fold increase in bladder, ovarian, and thyroid cancers. When looking at birth defects, there was also a significant increase in spina bifida, limb deficiencies and cleft palate, even given the mothers consumed the water with nitrates only a few months. In all animal studies, it was shown that water with elevated nitrates was a carcinogen. There are significant public policy implications of this work - listen to the podcast for Dr. Weyer's take on this. texts.full_text: by Mikhail Ruderman and Ilya Vershinin. Plot In 1942, both Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union are gathering forces and supplies in one particular sector of the Eastern Front for a major attack. By night, the Soviets are secretly constructing a bridge across a river. To avoid detection, it is being built underwater, just below the surface. In an attempt to find out where the Germans plan to strike, Colonel Semenov (George Macready) has a small paratrooper unit dropped behind enemy lines to attack a divisional headquarters and hopefully take an officer prisoner for interrogation. The local partisans, led by the wily Kostyuk (Roman Bohnen), provide a guide, Lisa Elenko (Marguerite Chapman). The attack succeeds at first. Alexei Kulkov (Paul Muni), one of the paratroopers, takes seven Germans prisoner in the basement. Then, just as Elenko brings him a message, German artillery hits the building, causing part of it to collapse. They are trapped in the spacious cellar. Kulkov and his prisoners must now decide how best they can survive until help arrives - if at all... Cast: - Paul Muni as Alexei Kulakov - Marguerite Chapman as Lisa Elenko - Roman Bohnen as Kostyuk - George Macready as Col. Semenov - Philip Van Zandt as Gruber - Michael Mark as Petro Background: the film is set during World War II on the Eastern Front, showcasing the intense conflict between Soviet and German forces in 1942. It highlights how both sides used guerrilla tactics like underwater bridge construction (Soviets) or deploying paratroopers behind enemy lines for intelligence gathering purposes(Germans). Themes: The movie explores themes of survival under extreme conditions, bravery amidst adversity & the cunning required to outwit an entrenched and numerically superior foe. It also touches upon how war can turn even enemies into reluctant allies when faced with a common threat;

texts.full_text: animal toxicology and the human testing of new drugs. Then it gives some reasons why improved quantitative variability information is likely to be important. better definition of the sources and magnitude of variability in susceptibility in the human population is a central issue for, (1) making more quantitative estimates of both cancer and non-cancer risks from occupational and1. Environmental sources, and for understanding variability in the response of human subjects in clinical trials of new drugs. 2. It is also crucial for (2) implementing the vision of the National Research Council's "Toxicity Testing in the 21st Century: A Vision and a Strategy," in which human exposure and human variability have major roles. The challenge is that variability is a fundamental and often frustrating feature of human biology. It is the source of the immense diversity in human physical characteristics, in the way humans respond to drugs, and in disease susceptibility. It is also a major reason for the lack of correlation between in vitro and in vivo results, between results in animals and humans, and between clinical results and outcomes for drugs in clinical use. in the past, the importance of human variability has often been underappreci pertencially for low-incidence diseases. For example, the carcinogenicity of a toxic chemical was typically assessed in a small group of animals, and if the incidence of tumors in treated animals was low, the results were often dismissed. In the case of human carcinogenicity, a typical study might have 60 people in a high-exposure group, and if 3 of them developed tumors, the results were often considered inconclusive. However, if the background incidence in the human population was 1%, then a 3% incidence in the high-exposure group would be a 3-fold increase, which is not trivial. With the development of powerful new high-throughput technologies, it is becoming feasible to obtain much better quantitative estimates of human variability. texts.full_text: and inhibitor of cyclin-dependent kinases (CDKs). This finding expands our understanding of genetic predispositions to pituitary adenomas. Familial isolated pituitary adenoma (FIPA) is a distinct entity characterized by the occurrence of two or more family members with non-MEN1-related pituitary tumors. A significant proportion of FIPAs are associated with mutations in the aryl hydrocarbon receptor-interacting protein gene (), which plays an important role in cell cycle regulation and apoptosis. The clinical presentation, management, and genetic counseling for familial cases differ from sporadic adenomas due to their higher risk of recurrence and more aggressive behavior. Early diagnosis through family history evaluation and targeted genetic testing can lead to better outcomes by enabling early intervention and personalized

texts.full_text: 12 on Ubuntu with Oracle Java version `8.0`. The error message indicates that the JVM option `-XX:maxPermSize=350m` is being ignored because it was removed in JDK versions starting from release "1.7" onward, including your current setup using OpenJDK or HotSpot VM. Here's a step-by-step guide to help you resolve this issue: ### Step 1: Update IntelliJ IDEA Configuration Since the `MaxPermSize` option is no longer supported and should be removed for Java versions starting from JDK8 (which includes Oracle JRE/JVM), we need to update your configuration file (`idea.vmoptions`) so it doesn't attempt setting `-XX:maxPermsize`. #### Locate Your VM Options File 1. Navigate into the IntelliJ IDEA directory where `vm options` are stored: ```bash cd /opt/intellij-ideac/etc/ ``` 2. Open or create a file named after your version, e.g., for Community Edition 12 it might be something like: ```plaintext idea64.vmoptions (or idea.vimopts if you're on JDK7) ``` #### Edit the VM Options File 1. Use any text editor to open this `vm options` configuration file: ```bash sudo nano /opt/intellij-ideac/bin/./<your-version>/bin/<idea64.vmoptions> ``` 2. Look for a line that contains `-XX:maxPermSize=350m`. If it exists, remove or comment out the entire line by adding `#` at its beginning. 3 Save and exit (`Ctrl + X`, then press 'Y' to confirm changes). ### Step 2: Verify JAVA_HOME Ensure your environment variables are

No. 707,732, now U.S. Pat. 4,097,357 describes a method of regenerating zinc from an alkaline zincate solution, wherein the solution is in contact with at least two electrodes connected to a D.C. generator so that zinc particles are deposited on the negative electrode and oxygen is evolved at the positive electrode, the method having alternating periods comprising a first period in which the solution is conveyed at a sufficiently low speed (or at zero speed) so that the zinc particles are deposited on the negative electrode and a second period in which the solution is conveyed ata sufficiently high speed so that the deposited zinc particles are washed off from the negative electrode and subsequently removed from the reactor, said first and second periods being repeated alternately so that during each complete cycle of operation there is an effective deposit of zinc on the negative electrode. The solution may optionally be treated with a baffle or hood-like construction to facilitate maintaining the desired low speed of movement at the negative electrode in the regenerative period, and also may contain a suitable buffering agent, e.g., sodium hydroxide, to help maintain the desired pH throughout the reactor, usually in the range about 10 to 13, particularly when recycling the solution through the system is practiced. This method allows for efficient regeneration of zinc from an alkaline zincate solution by creating a continuous cycle that alternates between deposition and removal phases, ensuring consistent zinc production while minimizing waste and maintaining optimal conditions within the reactor. texts.full_text: 487 specimens of fine-needle aspiration cytology of the breast were retrieved, ...and divided into two groups: 243 specimens were processed using the AMAPS method, and the remaining 244 specimens were processed using the AutoSmear system. The following parameters were assessed for each group: cellular yield, adequacy rate, background quality (including the presence of blood, inflammatory cells, and debris), and diagnostic accuracy. Methods: Fine-needle aspiration was performed under ultrasound guidance using a 23-gauge needle attached to a 10 mL syringe. The aspirated material was expelled into a tube containing the preservative solution for each respective system. For AMAPS, the sample was centrifuged and the pellet was resuspended in a cell dispersion solution before being smeared onto slides using an automated device. For AutoSmear, the sample underwent a similar process but utilized its proprietary solutions and equipment. Results: The AMAPS system demonstrated superior performance in several key areas: 1. Cellular Yield: The cellular yield was significantly higher with AMAPS compared to AutoSmear (p < 0.05). This ensured that more cells were available for microscopic examination, improving diagnostic accuracy. 2. This led to vWF being recognized as a major coagulation factor in platelet adhesion; its role was further clarified with the description of type II, or "pseudo" von Willebrand disease (vWD), where patients have high levels but normal function and structure of von Willebrand Factor protein. Since then several studies on vWF deficiency have been published and a range of distinct clinical phenotypes identified in association to these diseases. However, most reported cases only exhibit mild-to-moderate bleeding disorders. texts.full_text: n used as the provocative tests for coronary artery spasm. To examine the distribution of myocardial blood flow during exercise-induced and hyperventilation-induced attacks of coronary spasm, thallium-201 myocardial scintigraphy was performed in 47 patients with variant angina. The extent and severity scores and severity index (severity score/extent score) of scintigraphic perfusion defect were calculated. In 32 patients, anginal attack associated with ST elevation on the electrocardiogram was induced by exercise; in 23 patients, the attack was induced by hyperventilation. In patients with either anterior or inferior wall ischemia, both the extent and severity scores and severity index in the scintigram were significantly greater in exercise-induced anginal attack than those in hyperventilation-induced attack. In patients with simultaneous anterior and inferior wall ischemia, they tended to be greater in exercise-induced attack. In eight patients in whom angina was induced by both tests, the extent score was significantly greater in exercise-induced attack than that in hyperventilation-induced attack (14.8 ± 7.6 vs. 9.3 ± 5.0, p < 0.05), but there was no significant difference in severity score and severity index between the two attacks. texts.full_text: ts, having made the daring break from the life they had always known, took whatever jobs they could find at whatever wages were offered. I think the first thing taught about a capitalist economy is the law of supply and demand: the greater the supply is of something in respect to its demand, the cheaper the cost. The result of such a massive influx of desperate workers into the job market must have severely cut the bargaining power of the workers that were already living in this country. The initial success of industrialization promised AmericanWorkers better wages and working conditions but instead brought about exploitation as companies sought to maximize profits by paying low rates for labor, often targeting those from marginalized groups who had fewer options due their limited English proficiency or lack thereof familiarity with the language system within America’s rapidly changing economic landscape. In such context my father's character was a victim of his times and place; he did not have much choice but to accept whatever meager pay that these factories offered him in order for himself, as well other struggling families like them during those days ,to survive through the hard early twentieth century economy . Chavez, 77 N.M. 104, 419 P.2d 759 (1966), wherein we decided that the trial court had erred in dismissing proceedings to determine damages, if any, resulting to a interest in a business lease on state public lands because of action by the State Highway Commission condemning access thereto. Upon remand, the issue of damages was submitted to a referee. The referee awarded defendants-appellees $45,000 as just compensation for the taking of their interest. The State Highway Commission appeals from this award. The issue on appeal is whether the referee erred in including an element of damages for loss of goodwill. We find no error in the referee's award. The leasehold interest was taken entirely because of its location and access to a highway. texts.full_text: n August 21, 1917 in Buffalo, New York, to John H. Evans, an anesthesiologist, and his wife, Ellen Spring. He received his undergraduate degree from the University of Michigan in 1939 and his medical degree from the University of Buffalo in 1943. Career Evans served as a public health offiCer in Japan as part of his active duty service during World War II, where he became interested in the work of epidemiologist John R. Paul. He subsequently received a fellowship to study infectious mononucleosis with Paul at the Yale University School of Medicine. This experience deeply influenced his career, as he became a dedicated researcher in the epidemiology of infectious diseases, with a particular focus on the study of mononucleosis, rubella, and the epidemiology of infectious diseases in general. After his fellowship, Evans joined the Yale University School of Medicine and the Yale School of Public Health, where he spent the majority of his academic and research career. He was a pioneer in the study of the epidemiology of infectious diseases, and his research was instrumental in the understanding of the epidemiology of infectious diseases, the mechanisms of disease transmission, and the role of the environment in disease spread. He was a professor of epidemiology and, in the 1960s, was instrumental in the study of the epidemiology of the German measles (rubella) and the role of the disease in causing congenital rubella syndrome, a significant public health issue of the time. Situated just south of Aalst, the small village invited more interest from the Germans that usual during World War II because of its radio station hidden in the nearby forest. The shack was the only facility in the area capable of receiving long-distance radio messages from the UK and US, and the Germans were keen to intercept any communication. One lunchtime, as we sat around a small table at Louise's modest home on Kerkstraat, sipping coffee with her husband Rene by our side sharing anecdotes about their lives in Liedekerke during those tumultuous years under German occupation from 1940 to 1944. Further, Merlin is absent in brain tumors due to germline and somatic aberrations \[[@R32]\] while in breast cancer, its absence is attributed to Akt-mediated proteosomal degradation \[[@R33]\]. However, elevated levels of Merlin have been observed in some glioblastoma cells \[[@R7]\]. Thus far, investigations on Merlin have focused on its tumor suppressor role and the Merlin phosphorylation has been considered to either trigger its degradation (T230 and S315) or to inactivate its tumor suppressor function (S518). To this end, its open conformation was shown to interact with other proteins, such as Paxilin, βII-spectrin, ezrin 1, and HEI10 \[[@R18],[@R19],[@R20],[@R21],[@R22]\]. Our work provides first-time evidence demonstrating that S518-Merlin has oncogenic properties that are not only the result of inactivating Merlin's tumor suppressor role, but also an independent process involving the regulation of Notch1 and EGFR pathways. Surprisingly, we discovered higher levels of Merlin in parental cells and U251-S vs. U251-R cell subpopulations. Because this correlated with the presence of higher levels of S518-Merlin, Notch1 and EGFR, we investigated whether S518-Merlin could regulate these proteins affecting glioblastoma cell proliferation and tumorigenesis. texts.full_text: on her stomach. She said we stared at each oother for a while, as if knowing this bond would last forever. That first birthday, though I don't remember it, was memorable for everyone who witnessed my arrival - a tiny, soul determined to make her mark on the world. Over the years, birthdays became a celebration of not just another year older, but of love, laughter, and memories that bound us closer. Mom would always prepare homemade gifts for us, intertwining love with every stitch or brushstroke. Our excitement was palpable as we eagerly awaited our presents. For every birthday, she managed to create something magical - a quilted dollhouse complete with garden gnomes or an intricately drawn fairy tale world on canvas. She infusEd each creation with the essence of our dreams and imaginations. When I turned five, my mother decided that it was high time for me to have what she called 'proper parties'. So began a tradition where we hosted friends at our home. It started small - a cake, party games, some balloons.
